agents.system_prompt: You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README ![Metaflow_Logo_Horizontal_FullColor_Ribbon_Dark_RGB](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/763451/89453116-96a57e00-d713-11ea-9fa6-82b29d4d6eff.png) # Metaflow [Metaflow](https://metaflow.org) is a human-centric framework designed to help scientists and engineers **build and manage real-life AI and ML systems**. Serving teams of all sizes and scale, Metaflow streamlines the entire development lifecycle—from rapid prototyping in notebooks to reliable, maintainable production deployments—enabling teams to iterate quickly and deliver robust systems efficiently. Originally developed at [Netflix](https://netflixtechblog.com/open-sourcing-metaflow-a-human-centric-framework-for-data-science-fa72e04a5d9) and now supported by [Outerbounds](https://outerbounds.com), Metaflow is designed to boost the productivity for research and engineering teams working on [a wide variety of projects](https://netflixtechblog.com/supporting-diverse-ml-systems-at-netflix-2d2e6b6d205d), from classical statistics to state-of-the-art deep learning and foundation models. By unifying code, data, and compute at every stage, Metaflow ensures seamless, end-to-end management of real-world AI and ML systems. Today, Metaflow powers thousands of AI and ML experiences across a diverse array of companies, large and small, including Amazon, Doordash, Dyson, Goldman Sachs, Ramp, and [many others](ADOPTERS.md). At Netflix alone, Metaflow supports over 3000 AI and ML projects, executes hundreds of millions of data-intensive high-performance compute jobs processing petabytes of data and manages tens of petabytes of models and artifacts for hundreds of users across its AI, ML, data science, and engineering teams. ## From prototype to production (and back) Metaflow provides a simple and friendly pythonic [API](https://docs.metaflow.org) that covers foundational needs of AI and ML systems: 1. [Rapid local prototyping](https://docs.metaflow.org/metaflow/basics), [support for notebooks](https://docs.metaflow.org/metaflow/managing-flows/notebook-runs), and built-in support for [experiment tracking, versioning](https://docs.metaflow.org/metaflow/client) and [visualization](https://docs.metaflow.org/metaflow/visualizing-results). 2. [Effortlessly scale horizontally and vertically in your cloud](https://docs.metaflow.org/scaling/remote-tasks/introduction), utilizing both CPUs and GPUs, with [fast data access](https://docs.metaflow.org/scaling/data) for running [massive embarrassingly parallel](https://docs.metaflow.org/metaflow/basics#foreach) as well as [gang-scheduled](https://docs.metaflow.org/scaling/remote-tasks/distributed-computing) compute workloads [reliably](https://docs.metaflow.org/scaling/failures) and [efficiently](https://docs.metaflow.org/scaling/checkpoint/introduction). 3. [Easily manage dependencies](https://docs.metaflow.org/scaling/dependencies) and [deploy with one-click](https://docs.metaflow.org/production/intro ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

arguments.expected_information: Look for functions/classes that handle command injection, stub generation, or building CLI commands for runner; identify methods to modify.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file implements a programmatic Click-based API (MetaflowAPI) that mirrors the CLI. It dynamically extracts Click commands/groups from a Click collection, builds Python callables with accurate signatures, lazily loads and binds these callables to an API object, and constructs CLI invocation stubs (lists of strings) that represent how the CLI would be invoked.

### Architecture
- Top-level mapping and helpers (click_to_python_types, get_annotation, get_inspect_param_obj) convert Click parameter types to Python annotations and inspect.Parameter objects.
- Parameter extraction utilities (extract_all_params, get_inspect_param_obj) build argument/option signatures and metadata from Click Command/Group objects.
- Command extraction and stub generation functions (extract_command, extract_group, _lazy_load_command) build Python callables that represent commands and subcommands. These callables have dynamic inspect signatures (via __signature__, __annotations__, and __defaults__) so IDEs and tools can see the same API shape as the CLI.
- MetaflowAPI class represents chained API calls. MetaflowAPI.from_cli is the main entrypoint that registers the top-level command (e.g., start) and returns a Python function representing the start CLI command; the returned function constructs MetaflowAPI instances when invoked and instantiates subsequent command-methods lazily.
- extract_flow_class_from_file handles loading the user's flow file (with a module cache), locating the FlowSpec class, and returning it for flow-parameter processing.
- _method_sanity_check validates user-supplied arguments against the expected types and converts values for Click-style invocation; MetaflowAPI.execute composes the final CLI token list (stub).

Key interactions:
- from_cli loads and decorates the Click collection (via config_options_with_config_input and add_decorator_options) and then uses extract_all_params/extract_group/extract_command to build per-command callables.
- __getattr__ on the generated API type is wired to _lazy_load_command so attributes (subcommands) are created on-demand and cached on the instance.
- extract_command can inject flow-level parameters into a command if cmd_obj.has_flow_params is set, relying on extract_flow_class_from_file and MetaflowAPI._compute_flow_parameters for flow parameter resolution.

### Functions & Classes

- click_to_python_types — dict: maps Click param types (classes imported from vendor click and custom parameter classes) to Python types used for annotations (e.g., StringParamType -> str). Used by get_annotation to compute parameter annotations.

- _method_sanity_check(possible_arg_params, possible_opt_params, annotations, defaults, **kwargs) — function:
  - Validates that supplied kwargs correspond to defined Click args/options (raises ValueError on unknown argument).
  - Uses check_type from the vendored typeguard to ensure types match expected annotations (raises TypeError if mismatch).
  - Converts special types for Click consumption (JSON -> json string; config_value tuple handling for ConfigValue/dict).
  - Produces a dict with "args", "options", and "defaults" shaped for later CLI composition: method_params = {"args": {...}, "options": {...}, "defaults": defaults}.
  - Importance: central validation/conversion step before building chain elements; modifying this affects how user-provided values are validated and converted.

- _lazy_load_command(cli_collection, flow_parameters, _self, name) — function:
  - Resolves flow_parameters if it's a string by calling getattr(_self, flow_parameters)().
  - Uses cli_collection.get_command(None, name) to retrieve the Click command/group object.
  - If found, wraps it using extract_group or extract_command (as functools.partial) and binds the resulting callable to the API instance attribute (setattr(_self, name, result)).
  - Returns the bound callable; raises AttributeError if no command found.
  - Importance: implements lazy-loading of command methods and binds them to the API instance so subsequent attribute access is fast. This is where CLI collection commands are registered onto API instances at attribute access time.

- get_annotation(param: click.Parameter) -> Tuple[Type, bool] — function:
  - Maps a click.Parameter type (via click_to_python_types) to an annotation (and whether it's a varargs element).
  - Handles optional/multiple/nargs semantics to return types like Optional[py_type], Union[List[py_type], Tuple[py_type]], and marks varargs (nargs == -1).
  - Importance: ensures accurate type annotations (for signatures and type checking) reflecting Click semantics.

- get_inspect_param_obj(p: Union[click.Argument, click.Option], kind: str) — function:
  - Uses get_annotation to build an inspect.Parameter for use in dynamic function signatures.
  - Returns a tuple: (inspect.Parameter object, annotation used).
  - Importance: produces the inspect.Parameter objects used to form __signature__ for the generated methods.

- loaded_modules — dict: cache of modules loaded by extract_flow_class_from_file keyed by flow_file path. Used to avoid re-importing the same module repeatedly.

- extract_flow_class_from_file(flow_file: str) -> FlowSpec — function:
  - Validates the flow file path and temporarily modifies sys.path to import it.
  - Loads the module (using importlib.util.spec_from_file_location), caches the loaded module in loaded_modules, and inspects members to find a FlowSpec subclass (or CustomFlowDecorator wrapper's _flow_cls).
  - Raises MetaflowException if none or multiple FlowSpec classes are found.
  - Ensures sys.path is restored if it was changed.
  - Importance: locates and loads the user's FlowSpec to allow injection of flow-level parameters into the API — required for has_flow_params handling and for computing flow parameters when building method stubs.

- class MetaflowAPI(object):
  - __init__(self, parent=None, flow_cls=None, config_input=None, **kwargs) — method:
    - Initializes the API node, storing _parent, _chain (list with a dict mapping API name to kwargs), _flow_cls, _config_input, and a cache for computed parameters.
    - Importance: constructs nodes in a chain representing nested subcommand invocations.

  - parent(self) — property:
    - Returns _parent if set; otherwise returns None. Used by execute to walk parent chain.

  - chain(self) — property:
    - Returns self._chain. Contains the sequence of commands/params for this node.

  - name(self) — property:
    - Returns self._API_NAME (a class-level attribute set by from_cli or extract_group to indicate the CLI command name).

  - from_cli(cls, flow_file: str, cli_collection: Callable) -> Callable — classmethod:
    - Core entrypoint. Calls extract_flow_class_from_file to get the FlowSpec class.
    - Uses flow_context to call config_options_with_config_input(cli_collection) and add_decorator_options(cli_collection) — this wraps/decorates the Click collection with config-related options.
    - Builds a class_dict with:
      - "__module__": "metaflow"
      - "_API_NAME": flow_file
      - "_internal_getattr": functools.partial(_lazy_load_command, cli_collection, "_compute_flow_parameters")
      - "__getattr__": getattr_wrapper (wraps _internal_getattr in proper flow_context at attribute access)
    - Dynamically creates a new type (class) with name flow_file that inherits MetaflowAPI and has the class_dict. Sets to_return.__name__ = flow_file.
    - Calls extract_all_params(cli_collection) to prepare signature pieces.
    - Defines an inner function _method(_self, *args, **kwargs) that runs _method_sanity_check and returns an instance of the generated MetaflowAPI subclass (to_return) initialized with parent=None, flow_cls, and the method_params; this inner function is assigned to m and then has:
      - m.__name__ set to cli_collection.name
      - m.__doc__ from cli_collection.help
      - m.__signature__ set using inspect.signature(_method).replace(parameters=params_sigs.values())
      - m.__annotations__ set to annotations
      - m.__defaults__ set to tuple(defaults.values())
    - Finally returns m (a callable function representing the top-level start command).
    - Importance: this is where the top-level command is registered and a Python-callable stub representing the CLI entrypoint is created. The returned function is the "stub generator" for the runner API's start command; IDE-visible signatures are set here.

  - execute(self) -> List[str] — method:
    - Walks parent chain to collect all chained nodes, then flattens the chain into a list of CLI strings (components).
    - For each command in final_chain: appends command name and then expands args/options according to values; handles boolean flags, lists, tuple options, and skips None defaults.
    - Returns a list[str] representing the CLI invocation equivalent to chained API calls.
    - Importance: assembles the final CLI tokens (the stub) that will be used by the runner or printed; critical for the agent goal of finding where the CLI command invocations are constructed.

  - _compute_flow_parameters(self) — method:
    - Ensures this node represents a start API (must have _flow_cls and _config_input and be a root).
    - Uses caching (_cached_computed_parameters) to avoid recomputing.
    - If CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG is True, uses flow_context and processes config options from the initial chain entry (reads default and provided values for "datastore", "quiet", "config", and "config_value"), converts paths/dicts via ConvertPath.convert_value/ConvertDictOrStr.convert_value and then calls self._config_input.process_configs to apply them.
    - Calls self._flow_cls._process_config_decorators to let config decorators mutate parameters and obtain a possibly new flow class.
    - Collects non-config parameters from self._flow_cls._get_parameters() into the cached computed parameters and returns them.
    - Importance: resolves flow-level parameters (used when a command is annotated with has_flow_params). This is central to injecting flow parameters into Click commands and to any behavior that depends on config processing for signature generation.

- extract_all_params(cmd_obj: Union[click.Command, click.Group]) — function:
  - Iterates cmd_obj.params, classifying click.Argument and click.Option into separate OrderedDicts and building inspect.Parameter objects via get_inspect_param_obj.
  - Builds params_sigs OrderedDict preserving positional then keyword ordering.
  - Returns (params_sigs, arg_parameters, opt_parameters, annotations, defaults).
  - Importance: core helper used by from_cli, extract_group, and extract_command to construct method signatures (used to set __signature__/__annotations__ on generated callables).

- extract_group(cmd_obj: click.Group, flow_parameters: List[Parameter]) -> Callable — function:
  - Recursively builds a class_dict for the group: for each subcommand in cmd_obj.commands, if subcommand is a click.Group, inserts extract_group(sub_cmd_obj, flow_parameters) under sub_cmd_obj.name; if subcommand is a click.Command, inserts extract_command(sub_cmd_obj, flow_parameters).
  - Creates a new type named cmd_obj.name subclassing MetaflowAPI with the class_dict and sets resulting_class.__name__.
  - Uses extract_all_params(cmd_obj) to obtain param sigs/annotations/defaults.
  - Defines an inner _method(_self, *args, **kwargs) that runs _method_sanity_check and returns resulting_class(parent=_self, flow_cls=None, **method_params).
  - Assigns attributes to m, including __name__=cmd_obj.name, __doc__, __signature__, __annotations__, and __defaults__, then returns m.
  - Importance: builds nested API classes and callables that represent Click groups and subgroups. This is where subcommands are registered on parent API objects (via class_dict) when the group is turned into a MetaflowAPI subclass.

- extract_command(cmd_obj: click.Command, flow_parameters: List[Parameter]) -> Callable — function:
  - If getattr(cmd_obj, "has_flow_params", False) is true, prepends flow parameters (as click.Option instances) onto cmd_obj.params.
  - Calls extract_all_params(cmd_obj).
  - Defines an inner _method(_self, *args, **kwargs) that runs _method_sanity_check, appends {cmd_obj.name: method_params} to _self._chain, and then returns _self.execute().
  - Sets m.__name__ = cmd_obj.name and populates __doc__, __signature__, __annotations__, and __defaults__ and returns m.
  - Importance: constructs the final callable that represents an executable command; this callable is what ultimately appends the command to the chain and triggers compose/execute to produce the CLI token list. Note the explicit injection of flow parameters into cmd_obj.params when has_flow_params is set — that is the command-level injection point for flow-level parameters.

- The script-level block at bottom (if __name__ == "__main__") — demonstration usage:

### File Overview
This file implements a programmatic Click-based API (MetaflowAPI) that mirrors the CLI. It dynamically extracts Click commands/groups from a Click collection, builds Python callables with accurate signatures, lazily loads and binds these callables to an API object, and constructs CLI invocation stubs (lists of strings) that represent how the CLI would be invoked.

### Architecture
- Top-level mapping and helpers (click_to_python_types, get_annotation, get_inspect_param_obj) convert Click parameter types to Python annotations and inspect.Parameter objects.
- Parameter extraction utilities (extract_all_params, get_inspect_param_obj) build argument/option signatures and metadata from Click Command/Group objects.
- Command extraction and stub generation functions (extract_command, extract_group, _lazy_load_command) build Python callables that represent commands and subcommands. These callables have dynamic inspect signatures (via __signature__, __annotations__, and __defaults__) so IDEs and tools can see the same API shape as the CLI.
- MetaflowAPI class represents chained API calls. MetaflowAPI.from_cli is the main entrypoint that registers the top-level command (e.g., start) and returns a Python function representing the start CLI command; the returned function constructs MetaflowAPI instances when invoked and instantiates subsequent command-methods lazily.
- extract_flow_class_from_file handles loading the user's flow file (with a module cache), locating the FlowSpec class, and returning it for flow-parameter processing.
- _method_sanity_check validates user-supplied arguments against the expected types and converts values for Click-style invocation; MetaflowAPI.execute composes the final CLI token list (stub).

Key interactions:
- from_cli loads and decorates the Click collection (via config_options_with_config_input and add_decorator_options) and then uses extract_all_params/extract_group/extract_command to build per-command callables.
- __getattr__ on the generated API type is wired to _lazy_load_command so attributes (subcommands) are created on-demand and cached on the instance.
- extract_command can inject flow-level parameters into a command if cmd_obj.has_flow_params is set, relying on extract_flow_class_from_file and MetaflowAPI._compute_flow_parameters for flow parameter resolution.

### Functions & Classes

- click_to_python_types — dict: maps Click param types (classes imported from vendor click and custom parameter classes) to Python types used for annotations (e.g., StringParamType -> str). Used by get_annotation to compute parameter annotations.

- _method_sanity_check(possible_arg_params, possible_opt_params, annotations, defaults, **kwargs) — function:
  - Validates that supplied kwargs correspond to defined Click args/options (raises ValueError on unknown argument).
  - Uses check_type from the vendored typeguard to ensure types match expected annotations (raises TypeError if mismatch).
  - Converts special types for Click consumption (JSON -> json string; config_value tuple handling for ConfigValue/dict).
  - Produces a dict with "args", "options", and "defaults" shaped for later CLI composition: method_params = {"args": {...}, "options": {...}, "defaults": defaults}.
  - Importance: central validation/conversion step before building chain elements; modifying this affects how user-provided values are validated and converted.

- _lazy_load_command(cli_collection, flow_parameters, _self, name) — function:
  - Resolves flow_parameters if it's a string by calling getattr(_self, flow_parameters)().
  - Uses cli_collection.get_command(None, name) to retrieve the Click command/group object.
  - If found, wraps it using extract_group or extract_command (as functools.partial) and binds the resulting callable to the API instance attribute (setattr(_self, name, result)).
  - Returns the bound callable; raises AttributeError if no command found.
  - Importance: implements lazy-loading of command methods and binds them to the API instance so subsequent attribute access is fast. This is where CLI collection commands are registered onto API instances at attribute access time.

- get_annotation(param: click.Parameter) -> Tuple[Type, bool] — function:
  - Maps a click.Parameter type (via click_to_python_types) to an annotation (and whether it's a varargs element).
  - Handles optional/multiple/nargs semantics to return types like Optional[py_type], Union[List[py_type], Tuple[py_type]], and marks varargs (nargs == -1).
  - Importance: ensures accurate type annotations (for signatures and type checking) reflecting Click semantics.

- get_inspect_param_obj(p: Union[click.Argument, click.Option], kind: str) — function:
  - Uses get_annotation to build an inspect.Parameter for use in dynamic function signatures.
  - Returns a tuple: (inspect.Parameter object, annotation used).
  - Importance: produces the inspect.Parameter objects used to form __signature__ for the generated methods.

- loaded_modules — dict: cache of modules loaded by extract_flow_class_from_file keyed by flow_file path. Used to avoid re-importing the same module repeatedly.

- extract_flow_class_from_file(flow_file: str) -> FlowSpec — function:
  - Validates the flow file path and temporarily modifies sys.path to import it.
  - Loads the module (using importlib.util.spec_from_file_location), caches the loaded module in loaded_modules, and inspects members to find a FlowSpec subclass (or CustomFlowDecorator wrapper's _flow_cls).
  - Raises MetaflowException if none or multiple FlowSpec classes are found.
  - Ensures sys.path is restored if it was changed.
  - Importance: locates and loads the user's FlowSpec to allow injection of flow-level parameters into the API — required for has_flow_params handling and for computing flow parameters when building method stubs.

- class MetaflowAPI(object):
  - __init__(self, parent=None, flow_cls=None, config_input=None, **kwargs) — method:
    - Initializes the API node, storing _parent, _chain (list with a dict mapping API name to kwargs), _flow_cls, _config_input, and a cache for computed parameters.
    - Importance: constructs nodes in a chain representing nested subcommand invocations.

  - parent(self) — property:
    - Returns _parent if set; otherwise returns None. Used by execute to walk parent chain.

  - chain(self) — property:
    - Returns self._chain. Contains the sequence of commands/params for this node.

  - name(self) — property:
    - Returns self._API_NAME (a class-level attribute set by from_cli or extract_group to indicate the CLI command name).

  - from_cli(cls, flow_file: str, cli_collection: Callable) -> Callable — classmethod:
    - Core entrypoint. Calls extract_flow_class_from_file to get the FlowSpec class.
    - Uses flow_context to call config_options_with_config_input(cli_collection) and add_decorator_options(cli_collection) — this wraps/decorates the Click collection with config-related options.
    - Builds a class_dict with:
      - "__module__": "metaflow"
      - "_API_NAME": flow_file
      - "_internal_getattr": functools.partial(_lazy_load_command, cli_collection, "_compute_flow_parameters")
      - "__getattr__": getattr_wrapper (wraps _internal_getattr in proper flow_context at attribute access)
    - Dynamically creates a new type (class) with name flow_file that inherits MetaflowAPI and has the class_dict. Sets to_return.__name__ = flow_file.
    - Calls extract_all_params(cli_collection) to prepare signature pieces.
    - Defines an inner function _method(_self, *args, **kwargs) that runs _method_sanity_check and returns an instance of the generated MetaflowAPI subclass (to_return) initialized with parent=None, flow_cls, and the method_params; this inner function is assigned to m and then has:
      - m.__name__ set to cli_collection.name
      - m.__doc__ from cli_collection.help
      - m.__signature__ set using inspect.signature(_method).replace(parameters=params_sigs.values())
      - m.__annotations__ set to annotations
      - m.__defaults__ set to tuple(defaults.values())
    - Finally returns m (a callable function representing the top-level start command).
    - Importance: this is where the top-level command is registered and a Python-callable stub representing the CLI entrypoint is created. The returned function is the "stub generator" for the runner API's start command; IDE-visible signatures are set here.

  - execute(self) -> List[str] — method:
    - Walks parent chain to collect all chained nodes, then flattens the chain into a list of CLI strings (components).
    - For each command in final_chain: appends command name and then expands args/options according to values; handles boolean flags, lists, tuple options, and skips None defaults.
    - Returns a list[str] representing the CLI invocation equivalent to chained API calls.
    - Importance: assembles the final CLI tokens (the stub) that will be used by the runner or printed; critical for the agent goal of finding where the CLI command invocations are constructed.

  - _compute_flow_parameters(self) — method:
    - Ensures this node represents a start API (must have _flow_cls and _config_input and be a root).

import importlib
import os
import sys
import json

from typing import Dict, Iterator, Optional, Tuple

from metaflow import Run

from metaflow.metaflow_config import CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG

from metaflow.plugins import get_runner_cli

from .utils import (
    temporary_fifo,
    handle_timeout,
    async_handle_timeout,
    with_dir,
)
from .subprocess_manager import CommandManager, SubprocessManager


class ExecutingRun(object):
    """
    This class contains a reference to a `metaflow.Run` object representing
    the currently executing or finished run, as well as metadata related
    to the process.

    `ExecutingRun` is returned by methods in `Runner` and `NBRunner`. It is not
    meant to be instantiated directly.

    This class works as a context manager, allowing you to use a pattern like
    ```python
    with Runner(...).run() as running:
        ...
    ```
    Note that you should use either this object as the context manager or
    `Runner`, not both in a nested manner.
    """

    def __init__(
        self, runner: "Runner", command_obj: CommandManager, run_obj: Run
    ) -> None:
        """
        Create a new ExecutingRun -- this should not be done by the user directly but
        instead user Runner.run()

        Parameters
        ----------
        runner : Runner
            Parent runner for this run.
        command_obj : CommandManager
            CommandManager containing the subprocess executing this run.
        run_obj : Run
            Run object corresponding to this run.
        """
        self.runner = runner
        self.command_obj = command_obj
        self.run = run_obj

    def __enter__(self) -> "ExecutingRun":
        return self

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
        self.runner.__exit__(exc_type, exc_value, traceback)

    async def wait(
        self, timeout: Optional[float] = None, stream: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> "ExecutingRun":
        """
        Wait for this run to finish, optionally with a timeout
        and optionally streaming its output.

        Note that this method is asynchronous and needs to be `await`ed.

        Parameters
        ----------
        timeout : float, optional, default None
            The maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the run to finish.
            If the timeout is reached, the run is terminated. If not specified, wait
            forever.
        stream : str, optional, default None
            If specified, the specified stream is printed to stdout. `stream` can
            be one of `stdout` or `stderr`.

        Returns
        -------
        ExecutingRun
            This object, allowing you to chain calls.
        """
        await self.command_obj.wait(timeout, stream)
        return self

    @property
    def returncode(self) -> Optional[int]:
        """
        Gets the return code of the underlying subprocess. A non-zero
        code indicates a failure, `None` a currently executing run.

        Returns
        -------
        Optional[int]
            The return code of the underlying subprocess.
        """
        return self.command_obj.process.returncode

    @property
    def status(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the status of the underlying subprocess that is responsible
        for executing the run.

        The return value is one of the following strings:
        - `timeout` indicates that the run timed out.
        - `running` indicates a currently executing run.
        - `failed` indicates a failed run.
        - `successful` indicates a successful run.

        Returns
        -------
        str
            The current status of the run.
        """
        if self.command_obj.timeout:
            return "timeout"
        elif self.command_obj.process.returncode is None:
            return "running"
        elif self.command_obj.process.returncode != 0:
            return "failed"
        else:
            return "successful"

    @property
    def stdout(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the current stdout of the run. If the run is finished, this will
        contain the entire stdout output. Otherwise, it will contain the
        stdout up until this point.

        Returns
        -------
        str
            The current snapshot of stdout.
        """
        with open(
            self.command_obj.log_files.get("stdout"), "r", encoding="utf-8"
        ) as fp:
            return fp.read()

    @property
    def stderr(self) -> str:
        """
        Returns the current stderr of the run. If the run is finished, this will
        contain the entire stderr output. Otherwise, it will contain the
        stderr up until this point.

        Returns
        -------
        str
            The current snapshot of stderr.
        """
        with open(
            self.command_obj.log_files.get("stderr"), "r", encoding="utf-8"
        ) as fp:
            return fp.read()

    async def stream_log(
        self, stream: str, position: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Iterator[Tuple[int, str]]:
        """
        Asynchronous iterator to stream logs from the subprocess line by line.

        Note that this method is asynchronous and needs to be `await`ed.

        Parameters
        ----------
        stream : str
            The stream to stream logs from. Can be one of `stdout` or `stderr`.
        position : int, optional, default None
            The position in the log file to start streaming from. If None, it starts
            from the beginning of the log file. This allows resuming streaming from
            a previously known position

        Yields
        ------
        Tuple[int, str]
            A tuple containing the position in the log file and the line read. The
            position returned can be used to feed into another `stream_logs` call
            for example.
        """
        async for position, line in self.command_obj.stream_log(stream, position):
            yield position, line


class RunnerMeta(type):
    def __new__(mcs, name, bases, dct):
        cls = super().__new__(mcs, name, bases, dct)

        def _injected_method(subcommand_name, runner_subcommand):
            def f(self, *args, **kwargs):
                return runner_subcommand(self, *args, **kwargs)

            f.__doc__ = runner_subcommand.__doc__ or ""
            f.__name__ = subcommand_name

            return f

        for runner_subcommand in get_runner_cli():
            method_name = runner_subcommand.name.replace("-", "_")
            setattr(cls, method_name, _injected_method(method_name, runner_subcommand))

        return cls


class Runner(metaclass=RunnerMeta):
    """
    Metaflow's Runner API that presents a programmatic interface
    to run flows and perform other operations either synchronously or asynchronously.
    The class expects a path to the flow file along with optional arguments
    that match top-level options on the command-line.

    This class works as a context manager, calling `cleanup()` to remove
    temporary files at exit.

    Example:
    ```python
    with Runner('slowflow.py', pylint=False) as runner:
        result = runner.run(alpha=5, tags=["abc", "def"], max_workers=5)
        print(result.run.finished)
    ```

    Parameters
    ----------
    flow_file : str
        Path to the flow file to run, relative to current directory.
    show_output : bool, default True
        Show the 'stdout' and 'stderr' to the console by default,
        Only applicable for synchronous 'run' and 'resume' functions.
    profile : str, optional, default None
        Metaflow profile to use to run this run. If not specified, the default
        profile is used (or the one already set using `METAFLOW_PROFILE`)
    env : Dict[str, str], optional, default None
        Additional environment variables to set for the Run. This overrides the
        environment set for this process.
    cwd : str, optional, default None
        The directory to run the subprocess in; if not specified, the current
        directory is used.
    file_read_timeout : int, default 3600
        The timeout until which we try to read the runner attribute file (in seconds).
    **kwargs : Any
        Additional arguments that you would pass to `python myflow.py` before
        the `run` command.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        flow_file: str,
        show_output: bool = True,
        profile: Optional[str] = None,
        env: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        file_read_timeout: int = 3600,
        **kwargs
    ):
        # these imports are required here and not at the top
        # since they interfere with the user defined Parameters
        # in the flow file, this is related to the ability of
        # importing 'Runner' directly i.e.
        #    from metaflow import Runner
        # This ability is made possible by the statement:
        # 'from .metaflow_runner import Runner' in '__init__.py'

        from metaflow.parameters import flow_context

        # Reload the CLI with an "empty" flow -- this will remove any configuration
        # and parameter options. They are re-added in from_cli (called below).
        to_reload = [
            "metaflow.cli",
            "metaflow.cli_components.run_cmds",
            "metaflow.cli_components.init_cmd",
        ]
        with flow_context(None):
            [
                importlib.reload(sys.modules[module])
                for module in to_reload
                if module in sys.modules
            ]

        from metaflow.cli import start
        from metaflow.runner.click_api import MetaflowAPI

        # Convert flow_file to absolute path if it's relative
        if not os.path.isabs(flow_file):
            self.flow_file = os.path.abspath(flow_file)
        else:
            self.flow_file = flow_file

        self.show_output = show_output

        self.env_vars = os.environ.copy()
        self.env_vars.update(env or {})
        if profile:
            self.env_vars["METAFLOW_PROFILE"] = profile

        self.cwd = cwd or os.getcwd()
        self.file_read_timeout = file_read_timeout
        self.spm = SubprocessManager()
        self.top_level_kwargs = kwargs
        self.api = MetaflowAPI.from_cli(self.flow_file, start)

    def __enter__(self) -> "Runner":
        return self

    async def __aenter__(self) -> "Runner":
        return self

    def __get_executing_run(self, attribute_file_fd, command_obj):
        content = handle_timeout(attribute_file_fd, command_obj, self.file_read_timeout)

        command_obj.sync_wait()

        content = json.loads(content)
        pathspec = "%s/%s" % (content.get("flow_name"), content.get("run_id"))

        # Set the correct metadata from the runner_attribute file corresponding to this run.
        metadata_for_flow = content.get("metadata")

        run_object = Run(
            pathspec, _namespace_check=False, _current_metadata=metadata_for_flow
        )
        return ExecutingRun(self, command_obj, run_object)

    async def __async_get_executing_run(self, attribute_file_fd, command_obj):
        content = await async_handle_timeout(
            attribute_file_fd, command_obj, self.file_read_timeout
        )
        content = json.loads(content)
        pathspec = "%s/%s" % (content.get("flow_name"), content.get("run_id"))

        # Set the correct metadata from the runner_attribute file corresponding to this run.
        metadata_for_flow = content.get("metadata")

        run_object = Run(
            pathspec, _namespace_check=False, _current_metadata=metadata_for_flow
        )
        return ExecutingRun(self, command_obj, run_object)

    def run(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun:
        """
        Blocking execution of the run. This method will wait until
        the run has completed execution.

        Parameters
        ----------
        **kwargs : Any
            Additional arguments that you would pass to `python myflow.py` after
            the `run` command, in particular, any parameters accepted by the flow.

        Returns
        -------
        ExecutingRun
            ExecutingRun containing the results of the run.
        """
        with temporary_fifo() as (attribute_file_path, attribute_file_fd):
            if CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG:
                with with_dir(self.cwd):
                    command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).run(
                        runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                    )
            else:
                command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).run(
                    runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                )

            pid = self.spm.run_command(
                [sys.executable, *command],
                env=self.env_vars,
                cwd=self.cwd,
                show_output=self.show_output,
            )
            command_obj = self.spm.get(pid)

            return self.__get_executing_run(attribute_file_fd, command_obj)

    def resume(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun:
        """
        Blocking resume execution of the run.
        This method will wait until the resumed run has completed execution.

        Parameters
        ----------
        **kwargs : Any
            Additional arguments that you would pass to `python ./myflow.py` after
            the `resume` command.

        Returns
        -------
        ExecutingRun
            ExecutingRun containing the results of the resumed run.
        """
        with temporary_fifo() as (attribute_file_path, attribute_file_fd):
            if CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG:
                with with_dir(self.cwd):
                    command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).resume(
                        runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                    )
            else:
                command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).resume(
                    runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                )

            pid = self.spm.run_command(
                [sys.executable, *command],
                env=self.env_vars,
                cwd=self.cwd,
                show_output=self.show_output,
            )
            command_obj = self.spm.get(pid)

            return self.__get_executing_run(attribute_file_fd, command_obj)

    async def async_run(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun:
        """
        Non-blocking execution of the run. This method will return as soon as the
        run has launched.

        Note that this method is asynchronous and needs to be `await`ed.

        Parameters
        ----------
        **kwargs : Any
            Additional arguments that you would pass to `python myflow.py` after
            the `run` command, in particular, any parameters accepted by the flow.

        Returns
        -------
        ExecutingRun
            ExecutingRun representing the run that was started.
        """
        with temporary_fifo() as (attribute_file_path, attribute_file_fd):
            if CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG:
                with with_dir(self.cwd):
                    command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).run(
                        runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                    )
            else:
                command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).run(
                    runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                )

            pid = await self.spm.async_run_command(
                [sys.executable, *command],
                env=self.env_vars,
                cwd=self.cwd,
            )
            command_obj = self.spm.get(pid)

            return await self.__async_get_executing_run(attribute_file_fd, command_obj)

    async def async_resume(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun:
        """
        Non-blocking resume execution of the run.
        This method will return as soon as the resume has launched.

        Note that this method is asynchronous and needs to be `await`ed.

        Parameters
        ----------
        **kwargs : Any
            Additional arguments that you would pass to `python myflow.py` after
            the `resume` command.

        Returns
        -------
        ExecutingRun
            ExecutingRun representing the resumed run that was started.
        """
        with temporary_fifo() as (attribute_file_path, attribute_file_fd):
            if CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG:
                with with_dir(self.cwd):
                    command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).resume(
                        runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                    )
            else:
                command = self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).resume(
                    runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs
                )

            pid = await self.spm.async_run_command(
                [sys.executable, *command],
                env=self.env_vars,
                cwd=self.cwd,
            )
            command_obj = self.spm.get(pid)

            return await self.__async_get_executing_run(attribute_file_fd, command_obj)

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
        self.spm.cleanup()

    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
        self.spm.cleanup()

    def cleanup(self):
        """
        Delete any temporary files created during execution.
        """
        self.spm.cleanup()

### File Overview
This file implements a programmatic Runner API for launching Metaflow flows as subprocesses and wrapping their lifecycle in a convenient ExecutingRun object. It builds subprocess commands using a Metaflow click_api stub (MetaflowAPI) and executes them with a SubprocessManager, reading back run metadata via a temporary FIFO.

### Architecture
- High-level: The file defines two primary classes, ExecutingRun (wraps a single run's lifecycle and logs) and Runner (constructs and launches runs). RunnerMeta is a metaclass that injects additional runner subcommand methods at class creation using get_runner_cli().
- Integration points:
  - MetaflowAPI.from_cli is used to create an API stub bound to a flow file and the CLI entrypoint `start`. The resulting `self.api` is called later to produce command-line argument lists (stubs) for commands like `.run()` and `.resume()`.
  - SubprocessManager is used to actually launch the subprocess spawned from the command list returned by the MetaflowAPI stub; it returns a CommandManager representing that subprocess.
  - A temporary FIFO (temporary_fifo) is passed to the child process as `runner_attribute_file` so that the child can write a JSON blob containing `flow_name`, `run_id`, and `metadata` which Runner reads back with handle_timeout / async_handle_timeout to build a metaflow.Run object.
  - CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG toggles whether the API invocation to generate the command is performed inside a with_dir(self.cwd) context.

### Functions & Classes
- ExecutingRun — Context object that represents a currently executing or finished run and the metadata required to inspect it. Important as the primary return type from Runner.run/resume/async_run/async_resume.
  - __init__(self, runner: "Runner", command_obj: CommandManager, run_obj: Run) -> None — Stores references to the parent Runner, the CommandManager for the subprocess, and the metaflow.Run object. Not intended to be constructed by users directly.
  - __enter__(self) -> "ExecutingRun" — Enter support for context manager use; returns self.
  - __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback) — Delegates exit handling to the parent Runner.__exit__.
  - wait(self, timeout: Optional[float] = None, stream: Optional[str] = None) -> "ExecutingRun" (async) — Awaits the underlying CommandManager.wait (with optional timeout and stream) and returns self. Used for asynchronous callers who want to wait for completion.
  - returncode (property) — Returns the subprocess returncode from command_obj.process.returncode (None if still running). Primary way to inspect completion status.
  - status (property) — Returns one of "timeout", "running", "failed", or "successful" based on command_obj timeout flag and process.returncode. Convenience for examining run outcome.
  - stdout (property) — Reads and returns the current contents of the stdout log file recorded by CommandManager. Allows programmatic inspection of stdout.
  - stderr (property) — Reads and returns the current contents of the stderr log file recorded by CommandManager. Allows programmatic inspection of stderr.
  - stream_log(self, stream: str, position: Optional[int] = None) -> Iterator[Tuple[int, str]] (async) — Delegates to command_obj.stream_log(...) and yields (position, line) pairs for incremental log streaming.

- RunnerMeta (metaclass) — On class creation, calls get_runner_cli() and injects methods onto the Runner class for each runner subcommand returned by get_runner_cli().
  - __new__(mcs, name, bases, dct) — Creates the class and then:
    - Iterates over runner_subcommand in get_runner_cli().
    - Converts runner_subcommand.name (hyphenated CLI name) to an underscore method_name.
    - Defines an injected method f that calls runner_subcommand(self, *args, **kwargs).
    - Copies docstring and sets f.__name__ = method_name.
    - Attaches f to the class.
    This injects programmatic wrappers for CLI subcommands; the code expects runner_subcommand to be callable and have `.name` and `__doc__`. This is an integration point with the click-based runner CLI stubs.

- Runner (metaclass=RunnerMeta) — Main public API used to programmatically run flows. Creates MetaflowAPI.from_cli stubs and uses them to build commands executed as subprocesses. Important for all uses that want to run/resume flows from Python.
  - __init__(self, flow_file: str, show_output: bool = True, profile: Optional[str] = None, env: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, cwd: Optional[str] = None, file_read_timeout: int = 3600, **kwargs) — Key initialization steps:
    - Reloads CLI modules under a flow_context(None) to remove any flow-specific parameter configuration (note: imports local to avoid interfering with user flow definitions).
    - Imports `start` from metaflow.cli and `MetaflowAPI` from metaflow.runner.click_api.
    - Normalizes flow_file to absolute path.
    - Sets environment variables (including METAFLOW_PROFILE if `profile` provided).
    - Creates SubprocessManager instance `self.spm`.
    - Stores top-level kwargs that map to top-level CLI options passed to the API stub.
    - Crucially: calls MetaflowAPI.from_cli(self.flow_file, start) and stores it as self.api — this is the primary consumption point for the click_api generated stubs.
  - __enter__(self) -> "Runner" — Context manager enter, returns self.
  - __aenter__(self) -> "Runner" (async) — Async context manager enter, returns self.
  - __get_executing_run(self, attribute_file_fd, command_obj) — Blocking helper that:
    - Uses handle_timeout(attribute_file_fd, command_obj, self.file_read_timeout) to read JSON content written to the FIFO by the launched process.
    - Calls command_obj.sync_wait() to wait for process completion (synchronous wait).
    - Parses JSON to extract flow_name/run_id and metadata and constructs a metaflow.Run via Run(pathspec, _namespace_check=False, _current_metadata=metadata_for_flow).
    - Returns an ExecutingRun(self, command_obj, run_object).
    Importance: translates the runner_attribute file into an in-process Run object and packages it with the subprocess manager.
  - __async_get_executing_run(self, attribute_file_fd, command_obj) — Async equivalent of __get_executing_run:
    - Uses async_handle_timeout to read FIFO.
    - Parses and constructs Run() similarly.
    - Returns ExecutingRun.
  - run(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun — Blocking run method:
    - Creates a temporary FIFO (temporary_fifo) giving attribute_file_path and attribute_file_fd.
    - Calls self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).run(runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path, **kwargs) to produce a `command` (list of strings). If CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG is true, the call is wrapped inside with_dir(self.cwd).
      - This is a primary usage of the click_api stub: the API object returned by MetaflowAPI.from_cli(...) is invoked to produce the command list for the child process. The returned `command` is expected to be a list of CLI tokens that will be appended to [sys.executable, ...].
    - Calls self.spm.run_command([sys.executable, *command], env=self.env_vars, cwd=self.cwd, show_output=self.show_output) to launch the process, obtains pid and CommandManager via self.spm.get(pid).
    - Calls and returns self.__get_executing_run(attribute_file_fd, command_obj).
    Relevance: This is where the runner consumes the generated stub output and passes `runner_attribute_file` into it; the stub must accept this argument and produce a token list fit for subprocess invocation.
  - resume(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun — Blocking resume method, same pattern as run but calls self.api(...).resume(...).
  - async_run(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun (async) — Non-blocking run:
    - Same command generation via self.api(...).run(...), but calls await self.spm.async_run_command([...]) to start process asynchronously, then awaits __async_get_executing_run.
  - async_resume(self, **kwargs) -> ExecutingRun (async) — Non-blocking resume; same pattern using .resume and async_run_command.
  - __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback) — Calls self.spm.cleanup() to clean temporary files / processes on exit.
  - __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback) — Async exit wrapper; also calls self.spm.cleanup().
  - cleanup(self) — Public method to call self.spm.cleanup() to delete temporary files.

Other functions / utilities (imported and used but not defined in this file):
- temporary_fifo — Used to create FIFO path and file descriptor to pass to child as `runner_attribute_file`.
- handle_timeout, async_handle_timeout — Used to read FIFO content with timeouts while monitoring the subprocess (CommandManager) for termination or timeout conditions.
- with_dir — Context manager used when CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG is set to generate the command within the desired working directory.
- SubprocessManager, CommandManager — Subprocess orchestration primitives used to run commands and stream/process logs.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent is checking how Metaflow click_api stubs are consumed and where command injection could occur. Relevant points in this file:
- MetaflowAPI.from_cli usage:
  - Runner.__init__: self.api = MetaflowAPI.from_cli(self.flow_file, start)
    - This is the exact integration point where the click_api-generated MetaflowAPI is created for the flow file and CLI entrypoint. The returned object is used to produce concrete commands.
- Generated-stub consumption:
  - Runner.run, Runner.resume, Runner.async_run, Runner.async_resume:
    - Each calls self.api(**self.top_level_kwargs).run(...) or .resume(...). These calls expect the generated stub to return a list of command tokens (strings) suitable to be expanded into subprocess arguments which are executed as [sys.executable, *command]. The stub must accept runner_attribute_file and any flow parameters and flags, and must return the command list in the expected format.
    - The code wraps the call in with_dir(self.cwd) when CLICK_API_PROCESS_CONFIG is truthy — this influences the environment/context in which the stub is invoked and therefore may change relative-path behavior or how it constructs the command tokens.
  - The runner passes runner_attribute_file=attribute_file_path to the stub; the child process (invoked using returned command tokens) is expected to write a JSON with flow_name, run_id and metadata to that path. Runner reads that FIFO with handle_timeout / async_handle_timeout to construct a metaflow.Run object. Thus the stub must ensure the launched subprocess writes this file using that exact parameter name and file format.
- RunnerMeta injection:
  - RunnerMeta.__new__ uses get_runner_cli() and attaches methods named after runner_subcommand.name (converted to underscores) that call runner_subcommand(self, ...). This is another integration with the CLI API: the runner_subcommand objects returned by get_runner_cli() are added as methods, and they will be invoked with self (Runner instance) — these can modify or extend Runner behavior but are separate from the MetaflowAPI.from_cli stub consumption. They indicate that the Runner class expects CLI subcommands to be callable with a Runner instance; their signature/behavior could influence how click_api-generated objects are expected to behave.
- Command execution expectations:
  - The returned `command` from the API stub is directly spliced into [sys.executable, *command] and passed to SubprocessManager.run_command / async_run_command. The stub must therefore return command tokens appropriate for a Python invocation (e.g., ["myflow.py", "run", "--some-flag", "value"] or possibly something starting with "-m", etc.). If the stub output contains untrusted content, it will be used as subprocess arguments.

In short, the file consumes click_api stubs at the points:
- MetaflowAPI.from_cli(...) in Runner.__init__
- self.api(...).run(...) and self.api(...).resume(...) in the run/resume/async_run/async_resume methods
and relies on the stub to:
- accept runner_attribute_file and other params
- return a list of command tokens to be executed via subprocess
- ensure the spawned process writes the runner_attribute_file JSON the Runner expects.
